summary.intent: Debug and fix browser session instability in a multi-session browser automation UI (moltis), specifically sessions dying on click and empty action history, then investigate a freeze/hang reported during scroll while switching sessions.
summary.outcome: Two root causes were fixed and pushed: action name matching changed from exact match to starts_with() for mouse_input, and action hook registration was moved inside the manager init closure to fix lazy-init ordering. A third bug (relay task accumulation on session switch) was identified and fixed by skipping start_screencast when a session already has screencasting active.

repo: Action names in moltis_browser are formatted as 'mouse_input(x=..., y=...)' — not bare 'mouse_input' — requiring starts_with() matching rather than exact equality.
The browser manager uses lazy initialization; hooks registered at startup against a not-yet-initialized manager are silently dropped. Hooks must be applied inside the manager() init closure.
Each call to start_screencast spawns a new WebSocket relay task; old relay tasks are not automatically stopped on session switch, causing relay task accumulation and UI freeze.
The sessions API reports 'screencasting: true' on a session, which can be used to guard against redundant start_screencast calls.
Sandbox execution mode uses browserless/chrome container image; sessions are identified by IDs like 'browser-XXXX'.
On shutdown, moltis_httpd closes the browser pool with a grace period (grace_secs=5) and stops all containers.

code.finding: Session switch logic was unconditionally calling start_screencast; fix adds a guard to skip if session already has screencasting:true, preventing relay task accumulation.

workflow: Log evidence of relay accumulation: multiple 'screencast started (viewport resized)' lines for the same session_id across session switches is the diagnostic signal.

The freeze produced no error logs — only INFO-level repeated get_url/evaluate polling with increasing gaps, making the relay accumulation non-obvious without reading the screencast startup logs carefully.

friction: Freeze had no error logs — only inferred from behavioral pattern in INFO logs, requiring careful log analysis.
Lazy manager initialization silently dropped hooks registered at startup, with no warning or error at hook registration time.
Action name format ('mouse_input(x=..., y=...)') was not documented or enforced in a way that made the exact-match bug obvious.
open_items: Relay tasks from previous session switches may still accumulate if start_screencast is called outside the guarded path — audit all screencast call sites.
No mechanism to explicitly stop/clean up a relay task when switching away from a session; rely cleanup is implicit only.
The third browser session ('browser-5ba88a24c3990c78') appears in shutdown logs but was not actively used in the logged actions — unclear if it was affected by the same bugs.
